title: What is Machine Learning?

slides.title: Definition
slides.content: Machine Learning is a subset of artificial intelligence...

learningTheories.name: Constructivism
learningTheories.description: Learning is an active process where learners construct their own understanding through experience and reflection.
learningTheories.application: In ML education, students build understanding by experimenting with algorithms and observing outcomes.

learningTheories.name: Cognitive Load Theory
learningTheories.description: Learning is optimized when cognitive load is managed effectively, focusing on intrinsic, extraneous, and germane load.
learningTheories.application: ML concepts are presented progressively, starting with simple algorithms before moving to complex neural networks.

concepts.name: Linear Algebra
concepts.description: Vector spaces, matrices, and linear transformations form the mathematical backbone of machine learning.
concepts.importance: Essential for understanding neural networks, PCA, and most optimization algorithms.

concepts.name: Statistics & Probability
concepts.description: Statistical inference and probability distributions are fundamental to understanding ML model behavior.
concepts.importance: Critical for model evaluation, uncertainty quantification, and Bayesian methods.

concepts.name: Bloom's Taxonomy
concepts.description: A framework for categorizing learning objectives from basic recall to creative application.
concepts.importance: Helps structure ML curriculum from memorizing algorithms to creating novel solutions.

concepts.name: Zone of Proximal Development
concepts.description: The difference between what a learner can do independently and with guidance.
concepts.importance: Guides the design of scaffolded learning experiences in complex ML topics.

learningObjectives: Understand learning theories in ML context

title: Types of Machine Learning

learningTheories.name: Behaviorism
learningTheories.description: Learning occurs through conditioning, reinforcement, and observable behavior changes.
learningTheories.application: Supervised learning mirrors behaviorist principles with feedback-driven learning from labeled examples.

learningTheories.name: Social Learning Theory
learningTheories.description: Learning occurs through observation, imitation, and modeling of others' behaviors.
learningTheories.application: Transfer learning in ML mimics how humans learn new tasks by building on previous knowledge.

concepts.name: Feature Learning
concepts.description: The process of automatically discovering representations needed for classification or detection.
concepts.importance: Core to understanding how neural networks learn hierarchical features.

concepts.name: Bias-Variance Tradeoff
concepts.description: The fundamental tension between model complexity and generalization ability.
concepts.importance: Essential for understanding overfitting and model selection.

learningObjectives: Apply learning theories to ML paradigms

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Linear regression is one of the simplest ML algorithms...

sections.title: Mathematical Foundation
sections.content: The linear regression equation: y = mx + b...

learningTheories.name: Cognitive Apprenticeship
learningTheories.description: Learning through expert modeling, scaffolding, and gradual release of responsibility.
learningTheories.application: Teaching linear regression through worked examples, guided practice, then independent implementation.

learningTheories.name: Experiential Learning
learningTheories.description: Learning through direct experience, reflection, abstract conceptualization, and active experimentation.
learningTheories.application: Students implement linear regression, observe results, reflect on performance, then modify approaches.

concepts.name: Gradient Descent
concepts.description: An iterative optimization algorithm for finding minimum of a function.
concepts.importance: Fundamental algorithm behind training most machine learning models.

concepts.name: Loss Functions
concepts.description: Mathematical functions that quantify the difference between predicted and actual values.
concepts.importance: Essential for understanding how models learn and improve performance.

concepts.name: Ordinary Least Squares
concepts.description: Method for estimating unknown parameters in linear regression by minimizing sum of squared residuals.
concepts.importance: Provides analytical solution for linear regression and foundation for understanding optimization.

questions.question: What type of learning uses labeled data?
questions.explanation: Supervised learning requires labeled training data.
